method_notes: All 14 leads verified present in candidates_dach_nl.csv (grep on company name; sector field taken from the CSV's FocusSector). Siemens AG itself, OMV, VAT Group, Daimler Truck, Lonza, Bosch are NOT in the candidate CSV and were therefore excluded despite being suggested examples.

Country spread: Germany 7 (Siemens Energy, BASF, Bayer, Merck KGaA, Infineon, thyssenkrupp, Schaeffler), Switzerland 3 (ABB, Sika, Novartis), Netherlands 3 (ASML, NXP, Philips), Austria 1 (voestalpine). Sector spread covers 8 of the 12 focus sectors: Electricals, Chemicals, Pharma, Semiconductors, Medical Devices, Specialty Steel, Shipbuilding/Marine (via thyssenkrupp Marine Systems), Auto/EV, Green Energy (via Siemens Energy/nucera).

9 annual-report PDFs were downloaded with curl (Mozilla/5.0 UA, ~1s apart) and parsed with pdftotext, then grepped for India context: Siemens Energy AR2024 (36 India mentions), Sika AR2024 (12), Novartis AR2024 (11), voestalpine AR2024/25 (15), Infineon AR2024 (7), thyssenkrupp AR2023/24 (56), ABB Integrated Report 2024 (7), BASF India Factsheet 2024 (13), Philips India AR2024-25. Quotes marked 'pdftotext-verified' are exact extracted sentences; others are marked UNVERIFIED (from press releases/news/search summaries).

EU-India FTA: none of the parsed annual reports contained an explicit reference to the EU-India Free Trade Agreement negotiations in the extracted India passages; FTA-linked commentary appears mainly in press coverage (e.g. Tata-ASML, P75I) rather than in the ARs themselves — noted as absent rather than invented.

Level rubric applied: HIGH = named India capex/expansion or listed India arm plus stated growth commitment in report/CEO statement; MEDIUM = material India operations or demand commentary but mixed/no new capex signal (Novartis is buying out its listed India arm while growing its Hyderabad GCC; thyssenkrupp is divesting industrial India assets while chasing P75I submarines and BHEL electrolyser localization); no NONE ratings because candidates were pre-screened for size/relevance.
site_access_issues: bayer.com annual-report PDF (bayer-annual-report-2024.pdf) returns an HTML anti-bot page to curl even with browser UA; bayer.in AGM transcript PDF likewise blocked (403 via WebFetch) — Bayer evidence sourced from bayer.in web pages and search snippets, marked UNVERIFIED
schaeffler.com group Annual Report 2024 PDF returns a 562-byte HTML stub to curl — fell back to Schaeffler India integrated AR microsite via search
results.philips.com (global AR24) and asml.com/careers/find-your-location return 404 to WebFetch; philips.co.in India AR PDF downloaded fine instead
ASML Annual Report 2024 Strategic report PDF parsed cleanly (43k words) but contains zero 'India' mentions — absence itself reported as evidence, India detail taken from 2025-26 press coverage

Merck KGaA AR is an HTML web-report (reports.merckgroup.com); the Electronics R&D sub-page fetched contains no India mention — the '68% of Electronics net sales from Asia-Pacific' figure comes from other AR pages surfaced via search and is marked UNVERIFIED
ABB group report was only obtainable via a third-party-mirrored signed URL (library.e.abb.com); official search.abb.com download links require JS
